case_title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al

docket_entries.description: CONTINUED FROM ASBESTOS STATUS CONFERENCE CALENDAR ON APR-15-2010 TO DEPT 206 ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE CALENDAR ON DEC-16-2010 AT 1:30 PM IN DEPT. 206. PLAINTIFF'S CCP 170.6 PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE AS TO JUDGE KAHN IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF PREVIOUSLY EXCERCISED THEIR CCP 170.6 CHALLENGE AS TO JUDGE MCBRIDE WHICH WAS ACCEPTED. THE COURT ORDERED THE WEDNESDAY'S ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE CONTINUED TO THE FOLLOWING DAY SO THAT IT WILL BE HEARD ON THE SAME DAY AS BRAYTON'S CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE. THE 8/18/10 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS CONTINUED TO 8/19/10. THE 10/20/10 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS CONTINUED TO 10/21/10. THE 12/15/10 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS CONTINUED TO 12/16/10. THE COURT CONFER WITH COUNSEL RE TIMELINES FOR DEMAND EXCHANGE. A HEARING IS SET FOR 5/13/10 AT 1:30P.M. IN DEPT. 206 FOR COMMENTS. RESPONSES ARE DUE BY 5/7/10 AND MAY BE FILED UNDER THE COMPLEX ASBESTOS LITIGATION, CASE #828684. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: D. LOK (206) COURT REPORTER KIM RICHARDSON, CSR #5915, IS PRESENT FOR DESIGNATED DEFENSE'S COUNSEL, BERRY & BERRY.

ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANT PRATT & WHITNEY HAC INC.'S DEMURRER IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 8TH AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS SPECIFIED THE PRODUCTS THEY ALLEGE DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO IN A MANNER CONSISTENT WITH THE PLEADING REQUIREMENTS OF BOCKRATH V. ALDRICH CHEMICAL CO., INC. (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 71. THE DEMURRER AS TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. EACH PARTY CAN SUBMIT A LIST OF AUTHORITIES BY 5:00 P.M., 7/1/2010 VIA CONTESTASBESTOSTR@SFTC.ORG TO THE COURT. THE CASE WILL BE TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON 7/1/2010. MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Costs And Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Jun-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nicole Gage, Esq., of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2209), appearing for defendant / moving party Rockwell Automation, Inc.
J. Bruce Jackson, Esq., of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Donald Gauger, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR COSTS AND ATTORNEY FEES

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion for costs and attorney fees is denied. Defendant's notice of motion states that the basis for the requested award of costs is CCP 1032(b). An award of costs under this statute is determined not on a motion by the party seeking costs, but on a motion to tax costs filed by the party opposing an award of costs. Plaintiffs filed a motion to tax costs and thus the court will decide defendant's entitlement to costs under 1032(b) when ruling on the motion to tax. Defendant's request for fees is based on CCP 2033.420. That section only permits an award of fees against a party, not the party's counsel, and is based on the predicate that the party unreasonably denied a request for admission. (Estate of Manuel (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 400). No evidence has been submitted that any of the plaintiffs were aware of, much less participated in, the denials of RFAs 1-3, even assuming that those denials were unreasonable. There is no evidence that plaintiffs' responses to either the RFAs or similarly-worded special interrogatories were verified. 2033.420, by its language is intended to impose costs of proof on a party for the party's conduct, not the conduct of the party's counsel. A separate and independent basis for denial of the requested fees is the failure of defendant in its moving papers to provide any evidentiary support about the nature of the services for which the fees were incurred other than an insufficient conclusory sentence. While evidentiary support for the fees was provided with the reply papers, plaintiffs were denied the opportunity to provide written opposition to that evidentiary support. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sara G. Noel, Esq., of FILICE BROWN EASSA & McLEOD LLP (510-444-3131), appearing for defendant / moving party Raytheon Company
Cassiana Aaronson, Esq., of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Donald Gauger, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT RAYTHEON COMPANY'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Raytheon Company's motion for terminating sanctions is denied. Terminating sanctions are not commensurate with plaintiffs' failure to comply with the prior order. Defendant's alternate request for evidence sanctions is granted in part. Plaintiffs are precluded from introducing any evidence at trial or on a law and motion matter regarding any asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant that Mr. Gauger was exposed to that plaintiffs knew about or, through reasonably diligent investigation (but not discovery), would have learned about prior to December 1. Thus, plaintiffs can only establish product identification causation against defendant by evidence that plaintiffs did not know and couldn't have known through reasonably diligent investigation until December 1 or later. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Cassiana Aaronson, Esq. of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Donald Gauger, et al.
Linda C. Hsu, Esq. of BRYAN CAVE LLP (310-576-2192), appearing for defendant / opposing party The Boeing Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS MARGARET GAUGER, GAUGER CHRISTOPHER, CHARLES GAUGER's MOTION To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian of Records of The Boeing Company
No objection was made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to compel the deposition of the person most qualified of Boeing Company is continued to December 2, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220 to give the parties time to meet and confer in an effort to reach agreement on the scope of the deposition(s) and documents to be produced that is focused on OEM or Boeing-supplied replacement asbestos-containing parts of Boeing airplanes that it is reasonably possible that Mr. Gauger was exposed to during his career as a mechanic. As it is now written, the deposition notice is overbroad and imposes undue burden on Boeing. If any issues remain at that time, by 4:30pm on December 1, 2010 the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, what issues remain unresolved and then appear at the hearing at 9:00 am on December 2. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)

Allison J. Fairchild, Esq. of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON (415-342-7442), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Donald Gauger, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Admission; Request For Sanctions
Prior to the hearing the Court disclosed its pre-existing relationship with plaintiff. No objection was made. Parties were allowed to meet and confer. After discussion the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories and requests for admission is granted in part. The court only considered the issues addressed in defendant's reply brief. Pursuant to parties' agreement defendant's request for verifications to plaintiffs' supplemental responses to special interrogatories and requests for admission is moot. Issue as to special interrogatory no. 33 is moot. Defendant's requests for admissions nos. 5, 6, 7 and 10 are not deemed admitted. Defendant is awarded sanctions of $470 (calculated as 2 hours at $235 per hour) because plaintiffs lack substantial justification for their position. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Rita R. Lerner, CSR # 3179 (Tel: 818-609-9803) = (220/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nicole E. Gage, Esq. of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2105), appearing for defendant / moving party ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.
Cassiana Aaronson, Esq. of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Donald Gauger, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Argued and tentative ruling was adopted as modified: Defendant ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'s demurrer is taken under submission in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is overruled as to the 1st, 2nd, 8th and 9th causes of action because plaintiffs specified the products they allege decedent was exposed to in a manner consistent with the pleading requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. The demurrer as to the 3rd and 4th causes of action is taken under submission. Each Party can submit a list of authorities by 5:00 p.m., 7/1/2010 via ContestAsbestosTR@sftc.org to the Court. The case will be taken under submission on 7/1/2010. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
